reviews.review_text: This game helped me get a job as a detective. Still looking for the impostor at work though...

I do recommend this game to play with families and friends, but with strangers, it's another experience.
If you host a lobby, you should not be able to get vote kicked by others. I, the host of a lobby, was vote kicked because supposedly I set the speed too low.
"We're running a fckin snail marathon, mate." 
"My grandpa can fck faster than this."
It's my lobby. My rules, and if others don't enjoy it, they can leave and find another to their liking. I do not recommend this with strangers because very often, you'll find those who believe the world revolves around them, and worst of it, everyone tags along against you. I was also playing with my younger sister who had just witnessed me getting kicked out of my own lobby. The fix that I want to this game is that the host cannot be vote-kicked whatsoever.
Please, PLEASE, fix this, for the sake of me and my sister's sanity, and probably thousands, if not, millions more.

while the quality of the demographic could definitely improve the experience of this game, playing in the tight-knit discord communites so very present in among us is extremely fun and is what makes this game enjoyable

So, I've only played for 2 hours, but this game is super fun. I do have one big complaint, though. The players. I've already been told very rude things and have seen people with slurs in their username. I have also had offensive slurs typed to myself all because of a game. My rating though is 8/10. Very enjoyable, but the toxcicity is a big problem.

Among Us is one of the most engaging and memorable multiplayer games I have ever played. Despite its simple appearance, the game offers a surprisingly deep social experience that encourages teamwork, communication, critical thinking, and strategy.

One of the greatest strengths of Among Us is how it brings people together. Every match creates unique situations where players must work as a team to complete tasks while also trying to determine who can be trusted. The discussions and debates that take place during meetings are often intense, exciting, and genuinely entertaining.

The game's design is easy to understand, making it accessible to players of all skill levels. At the same time, mastering deception, observation, and deduction provides a rewarding challenge that keeps the gameplay fresh even after many hours.

The developers have also done an excellent job supporting the game with new maps, roles, customization options, and quality-of-life improvements. These updates have helped maintain the game's popularity and replay value over time.

Overall, Among Us is a fantastic example of how a simple concept can create an incredibly compelling experience. Its combination of strategy, social interaction, and suspense makes it a standout title that continues to be enjoyable years after its release. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fun and meaningful multiplayer game.

# Trolling my whole class with Among Us
-# So today in school, my English teacher was having us do presentation in front of our class. For my presentation, I decided to troll my entire class by making my entire slideshow about the popular game, **Among Us.** I started off the presentation by showing the class the picture of the **Among Us imposter wearing sneakers** (it's a meme on google if you want to search for it). For some reason, **no one laughed at the meme.** My teacher told me "this isn't what your presentation is supposed to be about." I responded by yelling **"THE TEACHER IS SUS I SAW HER VENT"** and then **naruto running** around the room. Again, no one even **giggled.** I assume it's because it was forst bell and everyone was tired. My teacher said **"Please sit down."** But I wasn't about to give up. I made a last ditch effort to make everyone laugh. **I started to beatbox the Among Us theme song trap remix.** I was beatboxing it so well (i had practiced it at home), i was certain that everyone would burst out in laughter. Unfortunately, ***not a single person laughed.*** Everyone was staring at me, so I said **"You guys are all sussy, I'm gonna eject you".**

`Long story short, I ended up getting a bad grade on my presentation and I got a detention. However, it was worth it because I totally got to troll my entire class.`

The game is very interesting, but sometimes it happens that the 100% red traitor kicks you, and it seems like everyone should kick him in the next vote, but no, everyone starts thinking again who the traitor is. Otherwise, it's a very high-quality and excellent game.

Among Us has been a great game since day one 🌿
But lately the garden feels a bit overgrown — too many cheaters, teamers, and players who only want 3 impostors.
Feels like a bunch of impatient sprouts who just want the impostor role every round 🌾

On the bright side, the game runs perfectly at 1080p / 60 FPS, even on old office PCs — low‑maintenance like a hardy houseplant 🌱

Humanity spent centuries building cathedrals, landing on the Moon, and splitting the atom, only to arrive at the peak of entertainment: accusing your friends of being suspicious because they walked slightly too close to Electrical. Among Us is not merely a game. It is a masterpiece of psychological warfare, a social experiment, a test of friendship, and arguably the greatest achievement in human history.

When I first launched Among Us, I expected a simple party game. What I received instead was a transformative experience that fundamentally changed the way I perceive reality. Every task is crafted with the precision of a master artist. Every emergency meeting feels like a courtroom drama. Every vent is a gateway to endless possibilities.

The graphics are breathtaking. Some people claim the characters are just colorful beans. Those people fail to understand true artistic vision. The crewmates possess a level of visual perfection that rivals the finest works ever created. Their elegant movements, their iconic appearance, their tiny little legs carrying the fate of entire lobbies upon their shoulders. Absolute cinema.

The gameplay is flawless. As a crewmate, you experience the thrill of desperately trying to complete tasks while wondering which of your trusted allies is secretly plotting your downfall. As an impostor, you become a master strategist capable of orchestrating schemes so intricate they would make world-class chess grandmasters question their life choices.

The social deduction aspect is unparalleled. No other game has allowed me to witness someone commit a murder directly in front of five witnesses and still somehow convince the entire lobby that an innocent bystander was responsible. The level of persuasion, deception, and sheer confidence displayed in an average Among Us match should be studied by universities.

The soundtrack deserves its own museum. Every sound effect carries emotional weight. The emergency meeting button alone generates more excitement than most blockbuster movies. Hearing that alarm instantly activates ancient survival instincts buried deep within the human brain.

The replayability is infinite. I have played matches where nobody spoke. I have played matches where people delivered speeches longer than presidential addresses. I have seen friendships formed, friendships destroyed, and friendships rebuilt entirely because of events that occurred in a fictional spaceship populated by colorful beans.

Among Us transcends gaming itself. It is philosophy. It is psychology. It is art. It is culture. It is the reason words like "sus" became part of everyday language. Few games can claim they altered global vocabulary. Among Us did it effortlessly.

Every second spent in Among Us is a second spent experiencing peak entertainment. Whether you're solving tasks, calling emergency meetings, defending yourself against outrageous accusations, or watching your best friend somehow convince the lobby that oxygen is a government conspiracy, the game never fails to deliver unforgettable moments.

10/10. I entered expecting a game. I left with enlightenment.

I mean, what can you expect from a game called Among Us?

I've been voted out for "faking tasks" even though visual tasks are on, I've cut a guy in half infront of half the lobby and still won the game, and I've even done nothing but tasks as an imposter and still been voted out.

If you have a delicate setup, dont play. Its 100% gonna make you rage in your early levels but once you get used to the game and start slowing things down, you become the person to vote out imposters who have done nothing but "tasks".
